selection_rule: min(_a18_encode_force, _a18_encode_force_v2, identity) by length per file

small-file mixed-JSON class — v2 startup amortization not reached, all files <16KB or v2-loses
anima large-file dominators (alm_r13_4gate_pass_subset etc) — text-heavy class with sufficient size where v2 amortizes

raw_91_c3_disclosure: This delta is measured on size-sorted top files within budget cap (2.5MB/repo). Same mix used to compute baseline 61.03% (v1-only) and 61.38% (min v1,v2,id). The delta is a pure A18 v2 inclusion delta — chain-orthogonal additive shift.

finding: v2 force-only is WORSE than v1 force-only on aggregate, even when restricted to ≥16KB files. Per-corpus: only hexa-lang (+0.41pp) and airgenome (+0.20pp) show net v2 force-only improvement. The min(v1,v2,id) selection rule recovers the wins (7 files / 402 total = 1.74%) without the regression cost.
raw_91_c3: v2 NEGATIVE on hive (-2.11pp), n6 (-1.34pp), nexus (-2.05pp), anima (-1.34pp) when forced; the 35f592c5 selftest fixture-6 finding (v2=189B > v1=178B on 234B JSON) is corroborated at production scale: order-1 model overhead dominates per-context predictability gain on heterogeneous JSON/audit corpora.

baseline_chain: A1+A17(AOT)+A18-v1(AOT) best-of-N {A1, A17, A18, A17+A18, A18+A17}
raw_91_c3_extrapolation_basis: v2 delta computed on apples-to-apples 6-repo file mix (61.03% v1 → 61.38% min, +0.348pp). Applied as additive delta to a6d36e1d 75.42% (which used same v1-only AOT). Extrapolation is conservative since: (a) a6d36e1d had access to A17+A18 chain not present in this measurement, (b) v2 wins overlap with A17 wins on some files (double-counting risk), (c) actual delta when stacked over A17 may be smaller than 0.35pp.
